title: OSX Cross ignoring non-zero exit building docs: unsupported options to grep and man
body:
### Issue Description

Noted recently, [example PR](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/20595/checks?check_run_id=19089961163) CI [task log](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/6449297361731584?logs=build_amd64#L204).  Not sure if this affects the official Mac packages or only our CI.  It may have secondary consequences though, like breaking the `--help` check on the Mac (if we run it there).

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Push to a PR, may use `[CI:DOCS]`.
2. Let CI run

### Describe the results you received

```
...snip...
go build -o build/go-md2man ./vendor/github.com/cpuguy83/go-md2man/v2
mkdir -p docs/build/man
grep: invalid option -- P
usage: grep [-abcdDEFGHhIiJLlMmnOopqRSsUVvwXxZz] [-A num] [-B num] [-C[num]]
	[-e pattern] [-f file] [--binary-files=value] [--color=when]
	[--context[=num]] [--directories=action] [--label] [--line-buffered]
	[--null] [pattern] [file ...]
/usr/bin/man: illegal option -- l
...snip...
```

### Describe the results you expected

`make podman-remote-release-darwin_amd64.zip` should complete without any errors.

### podman info output

```yaml
If you are unable to run podman info for any reason, please provide the podman version, operating system and its version and the architecture you are running.
```


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

I didn't debug this closely, but something in/under the `docs` make target is completely ignoring non-zero exits.

### Additional information

This fails on the Cirrus-CI provided Mac VM and [the (new) bare-metal EC2 macs](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/20691).

comments.body: I think we should either split out the validation step to another target that is only run in CI or add some `if linux` for the validation step. I guess we only need to run the validation in CI and for creating builds there is really no point in doing that step. @edsantiago WDYT?
In #20689 I conditionally disable `make docs` on Mac, precisely because of that. That PR has stalled, though. I cannot push it because I have no access to Macs.
Disabling the make docs step on macos seems wrong as it would mean it would not properly include the docs in the zip.

The docs end up in the release archive and I believe also the brew package.  So we need to at least test-build them on the mac.  Validating them can probably be skipped, since podman-remote == podman-remote, right?

@edsantiago poke me when you're available sometime, I'm happy to get you access to a mac.  Alternatively, wait for #20691 to merge, then you can do the "re-run in terminal" trick.
